data.title: CFTC Chair Predicts Legalization of Crypto Perpetual Contracts in the U.S.
data.description: CFTC Chair Michael S. Selig has indicated that genuine crypto perpetual contracts might be legalized in the United States within the next few weeks to a month. According to NS3.AI, Selig mentioned that the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is working on resolving classification issues related to perpetual contracts to replace the existing quasi-perpetual workarounds.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump on Iran: No Premature Withdrawal
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has stated that the United States will not make a premature withdrawal concerning the situation in Iran. According to NS3.AI, Trump's comments come amid ongoing tensions and discussions about the U.S. military presence in the region. The President emphasized the importance of strategic decision-making and maintaining stability in the Middle East. This stance reflects the administration's broader foreign policy approach, aiming to balance national security interests with diplomatic efforts.

data.title: Beeple's Robot Dogs With Billionaire Faces Roam Berlin Gallery
data.description: Digital artist Beeple has unveiled his latest installation, 'Regular Animals,' at the Neue Nationalgalerie in Berlin, featuring robot dogs with hyper-realistic silicone heads of famous figures like Elon Musk, Jeff Bezos, and Mark Zuckerberg. According to BeInCrypto, these autonomous robots roam the gallery, capturing images of visitors and generating AI art, which they print from their backsides. The installation, blending robotics, AI, and celebrity culture, also offers visitors free NFTs linked to the project. 'Regular Animals' will be on display until May 10, 2026.

data.title: Blockstream CEO Adam Back Sees Bitcoin as Future Economic Dominator
data.description: Blockstream CEO Adam Back has positioned Bitcoin treasury companies as arbitrage plays between the current fiat financial system and a future dominated by Bitcoin. According to BeInCrypto, Back suggests that firms buying Bitcoin today benefit from accelerating BTC adoption and the depreciation of fiat currencies. This creates substantial upside for early accumulators, positioning them as asymmetric bets on systemic transition. Back's thesis aligns with Michael Saylor's prediction of Bitcoin reaching $10 million per coin, though it faces skepticism from critics like Peter Schiff.

data.title: a16z Warns CFTC About State-Level Prediction Market Rules Impact
data.description: a16z has submitted an 18-page comment letter to the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) on May 1, expressing concerns over state-level prediction market regulations. According to NS3.AI, the firm warned that these rules could potentially drain liquidity and restrict user access. a16z highlighted that cease-and-desist orders and proposed state bans create obstacles that conflict with the CFTC's rules on fair market access. Additionally, a16z emphasized that blockchain-based prediction markets offer greater transparency due to the auditability of on-chain transactions.

data.title: Compromise Clarity Act Targets Stablecoin Yield Payments
data.description: The Compromise Clarity Act, unveiled on Friday, aims to prohibit stablecoin issuers from offering yield solely for holding payment stablecoins, while allowing certain activity-based rewards. According to NS3.AI, Senators Thom Tillis and Angela Alsobrooks released the text following extensive negotiations to differentiate crypto rewards from traditional bank deposit products. Cody Carbone from the Digital Chamber expressed optimism that the release could advance the bill towards a Senate Banking Committee markup.

data.title: Alberta Investment Management Corporation's Investment in MicroStrategy Yields Significant Gains
data.description: Alberta Investment Management Corporation (AIMCo) has disclosed its acquisition of approximately 1.38 million shares of MicroStrategy (MSTR) in the first quarter, with an investment amounting to $172 million. According to Odaily, the current market value of this holding has risen to approximately $241 million, reflecting a profit of around $69 million. AIMCo's average purchase price was about $125 per share. AIMCo previously held shares in the company from 2019 to 2020 but sold them shortly after MicroStrategy adopted a Bitcoin reserve strategy.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Defends Hormuz Strait Blockade
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump stated that the United States will not withdraw from the Strait of Hormuz, defending the blockade as a strong measure. According to Odaily, Trump asserted that the blockade is effective and predicted a significant drop in energy prices once the war concludes. "After this war ends, the prices of oil, gas, and everything else will plummet," he remarked. He also praised the U.S. stock market for reaching record highs and noted that projects during his administration are being completed "on time" and "on budget."

data.title: U.S. President Trump Ends Hostilities with Iran, Notifies Congress
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump informed Congress on Friday that hostilities with Iran concluded in April, allowing the White House to bypass congressional authorization. According to Odaily, under the 1973 War Powers Act, the U.S. President can deploy military forces overseas for up to 60 days without congressional approval. Trump's 60-day war authority expired on May 1. In his letter, Trump stated that the U.S. ceased military operations against Iran on April 7, following a temporary ceasefire agreement between the two nations, which has since been extended. If hostilities resume, the 60-day countdown will reset and begin anew—a legal interpretation likely to be challenged by Senate Democrats. "Since April 7, 2026, there has been no exchange of fire between U.S. forces and Iran. Hostilities that began on February 28, 2026, have now ended," Trump wrote in the letter.

data.title: Andreessen Horowitz Criticizes State Regulations on Prediction Markets
data.description:
Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) has expressed concerns to the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) regarding state-level regulations on prediction markets. According to Odaily, a16z argues that these regulations create 'barriers to fair access' and could potentially reduce market liquidity.

The firm highlighted that requiring platforms to restrict user access based on state laws affects market uniformity and conflicts with federal principles of fair access. Additionally, frequent bans and enforcement actions may diminish overall trading depth.

a16z emphasized the significant role of prediction markets in information discovery and probability pricing, advocating for unified federal regulation. Meanwhile, the CFTC maintains that related contracts fall under its exclusive regulatory jurisdiction.

data.title: Polymarket and Kalshi See Decline in Monthly Growth After Record April
data.description:
Prediction market platforms Polymarket and Kalshi reached a cumulative trading volume of $150 billion in April. According to Odaily, this milestone marks the end of a seven-month streak of monthly growth.

Data reveals that Polymarket's active trading users decreased from 733,000 in March to approximately 643,000, contributing to a decline in overall market trading volume. Despite this, Polymarket's U.S. operations continue to expand, and Kalshi's performance remains on an upward trajectory.

data.title: Canadian pension giant AIMCo bought Strategy at rock-bottom prices, realizing a profit of approximately $69 million.
data.description: Canadian asset management firm Alberta Investment Management Corporation (AIMCo) disclosed that it purchased approximately 1.38 million shares of Strategy (MSTR) in the first quarter, investing approximately $172 million. Based on the current share price of approximately $175, the holding is now worth approximately $241 million, representing a paper profit of approximately $69 million. AIMCo's average cost per share was approximately $125. AIMCo previously held shares in the company from 2019 to 2020, but liquidated its position shortly after the company shifted to a Bitcoin reserve strategy.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase: Increased stablecoin usage may not necessarily lead to a corresponding expansion in market capitalization.
data.description: JPMorgan analysis points out that while stablecoin trading volume is growing rapidly, its total market size may not expand proportionally, primarily due to increased velocity. The report states that as stablecoins are increasingly used in payment scenarios, the same unit of currency is used more frequently, improving overall efficiency and limiting demand for new stablecoin supply. The bank maintains a relatively cautious outlook, predicting a total stablecoin market capitalization of approximately $500 billion to $600 billion by 2028, believing that market expectations of a "trillion-dollar scale" are overly optimistic.

data.title: a16z supports the CFTC, stating that state regulations will weaken prediction market liquidity.
data.description: In its comment letter to the CFTC, Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) stated that state-level regulations on prediction markets are creating "barriers to fair access" and could weaken market liquidity. a16z pointed out that requiring platforms to restrict user access by state would affect market uniformity and conflict with federal principles of fair access. Furthermore, frequent bans and enforcement actions could also compress overall trading depth. The agency emphasized the important role of prediction markets in information discovery and probability pricing, and supported unified federal regulation. The CFTC, however, maintained that the relevant contracts fall under its exclusive regulatory purview.

data.title: Trump told the US Congress that the conflict with Iran was over, circumventing the war authorization process.
data.description: US President Donald Trump notified Congress on Friday that hostilities against Iran ended in April, a move intended to allow the White House to bypass congressional authorization. Under the War Powers Act of 1973, the US president can use military force overseas for up to 60 days without congressional authorization; Trump's 60-day war powers expire on May 1. Trump's letter claimed that the US ceased military action against Iran on April 7, when the two countries reached a temporary ceasefire agreement, which has since been extended. If hostilities resume, the 60-day countdown will reset and restart—a legal interpretation likely to be challenged by Senate Democrats. "Since April 7, 2026, there has been no exchange of fire between US forces and Iran. The hostilities that began on February 28, 2026, have now ended," Trump wrote in the letter. (Wall Street Journal)

data.title: US media: Iran eases conditions for resuming peace talks with the US
data.description: Iran has submitted a new proposal to Washington aimed at ending the war, showing signs of compromise and a desire to restart negotiations to resolve the deadlock that is taking a heavy toll on its economy. Sources familiar with the matter revealed that Iran's new proposal moves closer to the United States: it proposes to discuss Tehran's conditions for opening the Strait of Hormuz alongside a U.S. commitment to cease attacks and lift the blockade on Iranian ports. Previously, Iran had demanded that the U.S. lift the blockade as a prerequisite for starting negotiations and that the U.S. reach an agreement on the terms of ending the war before discussing the future management of the Strait and its nuclear program. Sources also indicated that the new proposal subsequently suggested discussing issues related to Iran's nuclear program in exchange for U.S. sanctions relief. Iran has indicated to mediators that it is prepared to travel to Pakistan early next week for talks if Washington is open to the new proposal. (Wall Street Journal)

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 78,000 USDT with a Narrowed 1.88% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On May 01, 2026, 20:41 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 78,000 USDT and is now trading at 77,951.570313 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 1.88% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Bitcoin Dominance Nears Death Cross, Ethereum Eyes $3,430
data.description: Bitcoin dominance is approaching a rare monthly death cross, a signal seen only twice before, according to BeInCrypto. Analyst Matthew Hyland notes that the slower yellow moving average is set to cross below the faster white moving average around June 2026, historically leading to sharp drops in dominance and rotations into altcoins. Meanwhile, Ethereum trades at $2,280, targeting a potential breakout to $3,430. The convergence of technical indicators suggests a possible altcoin season, with Ethereum's price action and Bitcoin's dominance level being key triggers to watch.

data.title: Ethereum Foundation Sells 10,000 ETH in OTC Deal to Fund Operations
data.description: The Ethereum Foundation has completed an over-the-counter (OTC) transaction, selling 10,000 ETH at an average price of $2,292.15. According to NS3.AI, BitMNR acted as the counterparty in this transaction. The foundation stated that the proceeds from the sale will be used to support its core operations. This recent sale is part of a series of OTC transactions, with the foundation's last three sales totaling 25,000 ETH, amounting to approximately $57 million.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Surpasses 2,300 USDT with a 1.54% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On May 01, 2026, 20:28 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has crossed the 2,300 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 2,300.409912 USDT, with a narrowed 1.54% increase in 24 hours.
